steps.title: Recognize the structure of the budget inequality
steps.content:
The inequality $9x + 13y \leq 104$ represents Jordan's total spending constraint.

Each term follows the pattern (unit price) × (quantity), where the product equals the total amount spent on that item type.

So each term in the left side must be a total spent on one of the pizza types.

steps.title: Identify what 13y represents
steps.content:
In $13y$, we have:
  • $y$ = the number of large pizzas (given in the problem)
  • $13$ = the cost per large pizza (from the structure of the inequality, this multiplies $y$)
  • Therefore $13y = 13 \times y$ = the total amount spent on large veggie pizzas
That confirms C is correct.
